title: glechoma schiraziana
intro: Glechoma schiraziana, also known as Iranian gypsophila or Iranian creeping thyme, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Iran. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Glechoma schiraziana include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in Iranian folk medicine. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional Iranian cuisine as a flavoring agent and garnish.
